topAlert.title: CISA: Microsoft SharePoint RCE flaw now actively exploited
topAlert.summary: CISA warned on Wednesday that attackers have begun exploiting a high-severity Microsoft SharePoint remote code execution vulnerability patched in May. [...]
topAlert.recommendedAction: Review immediately. Validate exposure, affected products, patch status, SIEM/EDR activity, and available indicators of compromise.
topAlert.businessImpact: Potential business impact includes unauthorized code execution, system compromise, privilege escalation, lateral movement, and service disruption.
topAlert.remediation:
1. Validate whether the affected product, vendor, service, or technology exists in the environment.
2. Review the original source article and any linked vendor advisory before taking action.
3. Identify the relevant CVE or vendor advisory and confirm affected versions.
4. Check patch availability, maintenance windows, and compensating controls.
5. Prioritize patching based on severity, exploitability, exposure, and business criticality.
6. Treat as urgent. Check CISA KEV/vendor guidance, confirm internet exposure, and search SIEM/EDR logs for related indicators.
7. If exposure is confirmed, consider emergency change control, temporary isolation, access restriction, or compensating controls.
14. Review Microsoft security guidance, patch status, Defender/EDR telemetry, and exposure of affected servers or services.
15. Document review results, remediation decision, owner, due date, and evidence for audit and incident-response records.
topAlert.remediationShort: Treat as urgent. Validate exposure, check for indicators of compromise, apply vendor mitigation, and patch or isolate affected systems.

alerts.title: FortiBleed credential-theft campaign linked to Lynx ransomware
alerts.summary: The massive FortiBleed credential theft campaign has been linked to the INC and Lynx ransomware operations, suggesting the stolen Fortinet credentials were intended to fuel future network intrusions. [...]
alerts.recommendedAction: Review today. Determine whether affected technology exists in the environment and prioritize remediation if exposure is confirmed.
alerts.businessImpact: Potential business impact includes operational disruption, data encryption, extortion risk, recovery cost, and possible production downtime.
alerts.remediation:
1. Validate whether the affected product, vendor, service, or technology exists in the environment.
2. Review the original source article and any linked vendor advisory before taking action.
8. Review EDR detections, recent authentication events, backup health, privileged account activity, and suspicious lateral movement.
9. Confirm immutable/offline backups are healthy before making major remediation changes.
10. Review impacted identities, enforce MFA, revoke suspicious sessions, reset affected credentials, and inspect mailbox rules.
11. Search email security logs and endpoint telemetry for related indicators.
12. Validate edge device firmware, external exposure, management interface access, VPN logs, and administrative login activity.
13. Restrict management interfaces to trusted networks and apply vendor mitigations if patching is not immediately possible.
15. Assign an owner and due date. Track remediation or compensating-control decision.
alerts.remediationShort: Prioritize containment readiness, EDR review, backup validation, exposed-service review, and rapid patching or mitigation if affected systems are present.

alerts.title: Cisco finally confirms attackers exploiting Unified CM flaw
alerts.summary: Cisco confirmed that attackers are now exploiting a Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) vulnerability patched in early June. [...]
alerts.recommendedAction: Review today. Determine whether affected technology exists in the environment and prioritize remediation if exposure is confirmed.
alerts.businessImpact: Potential business impact depends on exposure. Validate whether affected systems, vendors, or externally reachable services exist in the environment.
alerts.remediation:
1. Validate whether the affected product, vendor, service, or technology exists in the environment.
2. Review the original source article and any linked vendor advisory before taking action.
3. Identify the relevant CVE or vendor advisory and confirm affected versions.
4. Check patch availability, maintenance windows, and compensating controls.
5. Prioritize patching based on severity, exploitability, exposure, and business criticality.
12. Validate edge device firmware, external exposure, management interface access, VPN logs, and administrative login activity.
13. Restrict management interfaces to trusted networks and apply vendor mitigations if patching is not immediately possible.
15. Assign an owner and due date. Track remediation or compensating-control decision.

alerts.title: Medtronic notifies customers impacted by ShinyHunters data breach
alerts.summary: Healthcare device firm Medtronic is notifying affected customers about a data breach that exposed their personal data to an unauthorized third party. [...]
alerts.recommendedAction: Review today. Determine whether affected technology exists in the environment and prioritize remediation if exposure is confirmed.

1. Validate whether the affected product, vendor, service, or technology exists in the environment.
2. Review the original source article and any linked vendor advisory before taking action.
15. Assign an owner and due date. Track remediation or compensating-control decision.
alerts.remediationShort: Same-day review recommended. Confirm whether affected technology exists and assign remediation ownership.

alerts.title: ARToken PhaaS exposes EvilTokens' Microsoft 365 phishing toolkit
alerts.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform dubbed "ARToken" appears to operate as an affiliate of the EvilTokens phishing platform, giving researchers a glimpse into an extensive toolkit designed to compromise Microsoft 365. [...]
alerts.recommendedAction: Review Microsoft patch applicability and confirm endpoint and server update status.
alerts.businessImpact: Business impact appears informational unless affected technology is present in the environment.
alerts.remediation:
1. Validate whether the affected product, vendor, service, or technology exists in the environment.
2. Review the original source article and any linked vendor advisory before taking action.
10. Review impacted identities, enforce MFA, revoke suspicious sessions, reset affected credentials, and inspect mailbox rules.
11. Search email security logs and endpoint telemetry for related indicators.
14. Review Microsoft security guidance, patch status, Defender/EDR telemetry, and exposure of affected servers or services.
15. Track for awareness and validate applicability during normal security review.
alerts.remediationShort: Focus on identity protection: MFA verification, session revocation, password resets where needed, mailbox rule review, and sign-in log investigation.

alerts.title: ConsentFix and ClickFix: How Microsoft 365 Accounts are Hijacked in 3 Seconds
alerts.summary: ConsentFix and ClickFix attacks steal Microsoft 365 tokens in seconds using fake prompts and OAuth flows. Learn how these MFA bypass tactics work and how to defend against them. [...]

15. Track for awareness and validate applicability during normal security review.
